data.description:
## Why
`sign` and `verify-signature` (native signing commands, added in PR #4) advertise `[--output json]` in their help and emit structured JSON, but neither is registered in `rebar.schemas.OUTPUT_SCHEMAS`. This gap was masked by the old `tests/interfaces/test_schema_coverage.py::test_commands_advertising_output_have_a_schema`, which parsed the bash dispatcher's help arms (which predate the signing commands). Tier E7 rewired that test to the in-process help source of truth (`rebar._cli._help`), surfacing the gap; the two commands are currently in a documented exclusion set in that test referencing this ticket.

## What
- Author JSON Schemas under `src/rebar/schemas/` for the `sign` and `verify-signature` `--output json` payloads (matching the actual emitted shape).
- Register them in `rebar.schemas.OUTPUT_SCHEMAS` (keys `sign`, `verify_signature`).
- Add output-schema validation tests (mirror `test_schema_outputs.py`).
- Remove the `sign`/`verify-signature` exclusion from `test_schema_coverage.py::test_commands_advertising_output_have_a_schema`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `src/rebar/schemas/` has schema files for sign and verify-signature --output json
- [ ] `OUTPUT_SCHEMAS` registers both; `test_every_schema_file_is_wired` + `test_every_registry_entry_resolves` pass
- [ ] `--output json` payloads validate against the new schemas (added tests)
- [ ] the exclusion set in test_schema_coverage.py is removed and the test passes with no exclusions
data.title: Author + register output schemas for sign / verify-signature (--output json)
